data.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: FAIL**

---

### AC (original) 1 — Per-criterion eval set with the three case types, drawn from all three corpuses; prompt changes eval-gated.

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml`, `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml`, and `plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` exist. All three case types (recall `expect: finding`, false-fire `expect: pass`, discrimination pairs) are present. Cases are tagged by corpus (`corpus: dso`, `corpus: snap`, `corpus: rebar`). The eval specs are gated by `.github/workflows/eval.yml` (offline discipline every PR; live Inspect-AI run on workflow_dispatch).

---

### AC (original) 2 — ISF's eval cases (recall + false-fire) in the suite, gating ISF prompt changes.

**MET.**

`src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml` ships ISF-R1 and ISF-R2 (recall, `expect: finding`) and ISF-F1 and ISF-F2 (false-fire, `expect: pass`), with deterministic scorers `recall_on_silent_drop` and `no_fire_on_honored_or_justified_descope`. The eval CI gate covers these.

---

### AC (original) 3 — DSO stays read-only.

**MET.**

DSO is referenced only as a `corpus:` label on eval dataset cases. No write path to DSO is present anywhere in `src/rebar/llm/`.

---

### AC (added) 1 — The eval suite validates ISF (recall + false-fire) and tracks the observed-FP taxonomy (see eval/observed-false-positives.md) as labeled false-fires.

**MET.**

The finder spec seeds FP1 (T5c / domain-inappropriate-standard-import), FP4 (G6 / debatable-misinterpretation), FP5 (T5a / low-impact-nitpick), FP6 (T2 / library-guaranteed-capability), and FP7 (A1 / rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference) — all drawn from `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md`. The `README.plan-review.md` cites the full 11-mode taxonomy and the sycophancy axis. ISF recall + false-fire are covered in the ISF spec.

---

### AC (added) 2 — A T12 RECALL fixture: a plan that genuinely changes a DEPLOYED system's runtime behavior with NO staged-rollout/rollback path SHOULD fire T12.

**NOT MET.**

No T12 recall case exists in any of the three eval specs. A search for `criterion: T12` across `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/` returns zero results. The finder spec's dataset includes only recall cases for E2, E5, G5, and F4 — T12 is entirely absent from the standing eval suite as a recall fixture. The acceptance criterion explicitly requires T12 to be "validated for DISCRIMINATION, not only the suppression (not-applicable on libraries/CLIs) its calibration history covers."

---

### AC (added) 3 — Labeling (RESOLVED, no longer open): auto-labels seed the corpus for breadth; the GATING gold subset is HUMAN-ADJUDICATED.

**MET (marked RESOLVED in ticket).**

Each spec carries a `gold_set` (frozen, human-adjudicated subset for Cohen's kappa). `README.plan-review.md` explicitly states: "The labels shipped here are a best-effort SEED pending human adjudication: the bulk dataset is auto/seed-labeled for breadth, but the GATING gold subset (the cases that block a prompt change) is owned by a human reviewer." The ticket itself marks this criterion RESOLVED.
